articles.title: The 2028 Presidential Election Tightens To A Three-Horse Race At Kalshi, Polymarket
articles.summary: Prediction markets are already pricing in a three-way battle for the 2028 presidency, with betting action on Kalshi and Polymarket narrowing the field from a crowded early field to just three serious contenders. Early consolidation around frontrunners this far out suggests big money is betting on name recognition and party establishment picks, which could signal where the smart money sees inevitable nominee paths forming.

articles.title: Prediction Markets Up Against Illinois’ Century-Old Gambling Law in Series of Lawsuits
articles.summary: Illinois prediction market operators are battling the state's century-old gambling laws in court, challenging regulations that treat political betting like casino games. The outcome could set precedent for how other states regulate prediction markets, potentially opening or closing major US markets worth millions in trading volume.

articles.title: Faimon Roberts: Wanna bet on aliens? Prediction markets are the new, unregulated frontier for gamblers.
articles.summary: Prediction markets are now hosting bets on extraterrestrial life discovery, creating a wild new category that sits somewhere between science fiction and serious forecasting. This alien betting trend signals how prediction markets are expanding beyond traditional politics and economics into literally any question humans can imagine, potentially attracting a whole new crowd of speculators who care more about UFOs than unemployment rates.

articles.title: Future is unpredictable for prediction markets as courts add to confusion
articles.summary: Courts are issuing conflicting rulings on prediction market regulations, creating legal uncertainty across jurisdictions. The patchwork of decisions makes it harder for platforms to operate confidently and could scare off institutional money that prediction markets desperately need to grow.

articles.title: Kalshi ‘Death Bet’ Lawsuit Sparks Push To Ban Assassination Markets
articles.summary: Kalshi is facing legal action over markets that allegedly allow betting on political assassinations, triggering broader calls to prohibit such "death markets" entirely. The lawsuit could force regulators to draw clearer lines around what prediction markets can legally offer, potentially reshaping the industry's boundaries before it fully matures.

articles.title: US commodity regulator kicks off rulemaking for prediction markets
articles.summary: The CFTC is starting formal rulemaking to establish regulations for prediction markets operating under US jurisdiction. This could finally provide the regulatory clarity that platforms like Kalshi and PredictIt need to expand their offerings without constant legal uncertainty.

articles.title: Jail break, election betting: These bets are illegal in Louisiana. But the state can't stop them.
articles.summary: Louisiana bettors are placing illegal wagers on everything from prison escapes to election outcomes, and state authorities lack the enforcement tools to shut them down. This enforcement gap highlights how prediction markets are thriving in legal gray areas, potentially setting precedent for other states struggling to regulate political betting and unconventional wagering.

articles.title: CME, Cboe chiefs urge crackdown on fast-growing prediction markets
articles.summary: The heads of CME and Cboe are pushing regulators to crack down on the booming prediction markets industry. Traditional derivatives exchanges clearly see platforms like Kalshi and Polymarket as a threat to their monopoly on event-based trading, so they're trying to strangle the competition in the regulatory crib.

articles.title: Utah’s anti-gambling tradition meets Kalshi and Polymarket in a new legal fight
articles.summary: Utah's strict anti-gambling laws are colliding with prediction markets like Kalshi and Polymarket in what appears to be a new legal challenge. This fight could set precedent for how states with zero-tolerance gambling policies treat prediction markets, potentially creating a patchwork of access that mirrors sports betting's messy state-by-state rollout.

articles.title: Breakingviews - How insider-trading fears harm prediction markets
articles.summary: Reuters' Breakingviews argues that excessive worry about insider trading is holding back prediction markets from reaching their full potential. The fear creates regulatory hesitation and platform restrictions that ultimately hurt the price discovery mechanism that makes these markets valuable in the first place.

articles.title: Prime Brokers Race to Give Wall Street Access to Event Bets
articles.summary: Prime brokers are scrambling to offer their Wall Street clients access to prediction markets and event-driven betting platforms. This institutional rush validates prediction markets as a legitimate asset class and could pump serious liquidity into political, economic, and cultural event betting.

articles.title: Tesla Loses Director Behind Robotaxi Backend: What Do Prediction Markets Say?
articles.summary: Tesla's director overseeing the technical infrastructure for their autonomous taxi service has left the company, dealing another blow to their robotaxi ambitions. Prediction markets tracking Tesla's self-driving timeline and robotaxi launch dates will likely see increased volatility as bettors reassess whether Elon's promises are becoming even less realistic.
